title: Sequential degradation-state features via rolling slopes and residual resets
summary: Recent F1 tire modeling work uses latent state/degradation with pit-stop resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tire modeling work uses latent state/degradation with pit-stop resets. Your current features use cumulative degradation and lap deltas, but not the recent trend or deviation from expected stint trajectory, which is often what triggers the stop window.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x Driver`, sorted by lap, create pre-stop only features: rolling 2-5 lap slope of `LapTime (s)`, slope of `LapTime_Delta`, slope of `Cumulative_Degradation`, acceleration terms, and residuals versus driver-stint baseline after each pit reset. Prefer lagged/causal windows only.
expected_effect: Should help detect the undercut-ready or cliff phase that static tyre-life ratios miss, especially when compounds behave differently by circuit.
risk: If the rows are not strictly sorted within race/driver or if windows accidentally peek across pit resets, the features become noisy or invalid.
